citation: Pawloski et al. Clinical evaluation and validation of laboratory methods for the diagnosis of Bordetella pertussis infection. PLOS ONE, 2018.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Compares culture, PCR, IgG-PT serology, and clinical case definition by timing of cough onset.

citation: WHO. Surveillance guide for vaccine-preventable diseases: Module 5 Pertussis, 2023.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Recommends culture/PCR for acute infection; notes serology limitations and investigation/timeliness indicators.

citation: ECDC. Laboratory diagnosis and molecular surveillance of Bordetella pertussis, 2022.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Summarizes PCR, serology, culture, isolation, and molecular surveillance options.

citation: Kaczmarek, Ware, and Lambert. The contribution of PCR testing to influenza and pertussis notifications in Australia. Epidemiology and Infection, 2016.
key_evidence_for_dataset: After 1 Jan 2007 most pertussis notifications were PCR-based; by 2013 PCR largely replaced other methods except adult serology.

citation: Wylks, Ewald, and Guest. The epidemiology of pertussis in the Australian Capital Territory, 1999 to 2005. Communicable Diseases Intelligence, 2007.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Documents testing changes and false-positive serology concerns in an Australian jurisdiction.

citation: Quinn and McIntyre. Australian vaccine preventable disease epidemiological review series: pertussis, 2006-2012. Communicable Diseases Intelligence, 2014.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Reviews diagnostic methods recorded for Australian notifications during 2006-2012.

citation: Guimaraes et al. Increasing incidence of pertussis in Brazil: a retrospective study using surveillance data. BMC Infectious Diseases, 2015.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Brazilian surveillance 2007-2014 used clinical, epidemiologic, culture, and PCR criteria; lab confirmation was only part of confirmations.

citation: Oliveira et al. Laboratory identification of pertussis in the western region of Sao Paulo State, Brazil. Revista do Instituto Adolfo Lutz, 2017.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Regional laboratory identification used culture and qPCR, illustrating molecular availability but uneven coverage.

citation: PAHO. PAHO calls for strengthened vaccination and surveillance amid spread of antibiotic-resistant pertussis, 2025.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Regional alert likely increased awareness and testing in the Americas during the recent resurgence.

citation: China CDC Weekly. Reported cases and deaths of national notifiable infectious diseases - China, January 2024.
key_evidence_for_dataset: China NNDRS counts include clinically diagnosed and laboratory-confirmed cases.

citation: China CDC Weekly. Reported cases and deaths of national notifiable infectious diseases - China, January 2024.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Documents the large early-2024 notified pertussis counts before later enhanced-screening reports.

citation: Resurgence and atypical patterns of pertussis in China. 2024.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Reviews China's recent pertussis resurgence and atypical epidemiologic patterns.

citation: Chen et al. Incidence and trends of 17 notifiable bacterial infectious diseases in China, 2004-2019. BMC Infectious Diseases, 2023.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Documents long-run notifiable bacterial disease trends from the national reporting system.

citation: Frontiers in Public Health. Epidemiological and spatial-temporal clustering characteristics of pertussis in Southwest China, 2013-2024.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Discusses 2017 diagnostic guideline effects and enhanced surveillance sensitivity during the 2024 surge.

citation: Hu et al. Waning immunity, prevailing non-vaccine type ptxP3 and macrolide-resistant strains in the 2024 pertussis outbreak in China. The Lancet Microbe, 2025.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Reports enhanced active PCR screening from late March 2024 among medically attended children.

citation: Underestimated incidence rate of pertussis in the community: results from active population-based surveillance in Yiwu, China. 2024.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Active surveillance used standardized testing, culture, and PCR and found passive surveillance under-ascertainment.

citation: NIID IASR. Pertussis in Japan, as of November 2018. Infectious Agents Surveillance Report, 2019.
key_evidence_for_dataset: From 2018 all medical facilities notified pertussis and laboratory diagnosis was required as a rule; LAMP became insured in Nov 2016.

citation: Kitaoka et al. Laboratory-based surveillance of pertussis using multitarget real-time PCR in Japan. 2016.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Multitarget PCR surveillance in 2013-2014; notes NAAT was not yet widely introduced in Japan.

citation: Health New Zealand. Pertussis communicable disease control manual.
key_evidence_for_dataset: PCR is recommended; culture is not routine; most laboratories do not offer pertussis serology.

citation: New Zealand Microbiology Network. Recommendations for diagnostic testing requests, 2017.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Recommends PCR for acute pertussis diagnosis and discourages acute-care pertussis serology.

citation: Increasing incidence of life-threatening pertussis: a retrospective cohort study in New Zealand. 2016.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Notes New Zealand pertussis became notifiable and PCR diagnosis became available in 1997.

citation: Health New Zealand. Immunisation Handbook: Pertussis.
key_evidence_for_dataset: States national passive surveillance has been available since 1996 and PCR/serology improved confirmation.

citation: NICD. Guidelines for pertussis diagnosis, management and public health response, 2017.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Describes South African notification and diagnostic context.

citation: Wolter et al. Epidemiology of pertussis in individuals of all ages hospitalized with respiratory illness in South Africa, January 2013-December 2018. Clinical Infectious Diseases, 2021.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Prospective sentinel SRI surveillance tested specimens by PCR for B. pertussis.

citation: du Plessis et al. Risk factors for pertussis among hospitalized children in a high HIV prevalence setting, South Africa. International Journal of Infectious Diseases, 2018.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Hospital sentinel surveillance collected nasopharyngeal specimens for multiplex PCR and culture.

citation: South African Government. National Health Act regulations for surveillance and control of notifiable medical conditions.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Pertussis is listed as a notifiable medical condition; diagnosed cases are reportable.

citation: Genomic characterization of Bordetella pertussis in South Africa, 2015-2019. 2023.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Describes PCR-based sentinel pneumonia surveillance and genomic follow-up.

citation: Hallander. Microbiological and serological diagnosis of pertussis. Clinical Infectious Diseases, 1999.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Swedish studies show optimized culture/serology contributions and PCR added detections beyond culture.

citation: Gustafsson et al. Control of pertussis - lessons learnt from a 10-year surveillance programme in Sweden. Vaccine, 2009.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Describes Sweden's post-vaccine-restart pertussis surveillance programme.

citation: Nilsson et al. Surveillance of infant pertussis in Sweden 1998-2012. Eurosurveillance, 2015.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Documents infant pertussis surveillance after the national vaccination programme.

citation: Siriyakorn et al. Adult pertussis is an unrecognized public health problem in Thailand. BMC Infectious Diseases, 2016.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Notes adult diagnosis is difficult and laboratory diagnosis such as culture/PCR was often unavailable.

citation: Phiriyasart et al. Toward optimizing pertussis detection during outbreaks: comparison of national and high epidemic area case definitions in Narathiwat, Thailand, 2024. OSIR Journal, 2025.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Compares national cough >=2 week definition with high-epidemic cough >=1 week definition and RT-PCR confirmation.

citation: Modelling pertussis in Thailand's southernmost provinces. BMC Public Health, 2025.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Uses 2015-2024 southern Thailand surveillance data and highlights 2023-2024 outbreak hotspots.

citation: Amirthalingam et al. Accelerating control of pertussis in England and Wales. Emerging Infectious Diseases, 2012.
key_evidence_for_dataset: England/Wales lab confirmations added serology in July 2001 and PCR in November 2001; adolescent/adult confirmations rose without matching hospitalization increases.

citation: Fry et al. Laboratory diagnosis of pertussis infections: the role of PCR and serology. 2002.
key_evidence_for_dataset: PCR and serology enhanced surveillance across age groups.

citation: Public Health England. Laboratory confirmed cases of pertussis reported to the enhanced pertussis surveillance programme in England, 2014.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Enhanced surveillance counted culture, PCR, serology, or oral-fluid laboratory confirmations.

citation: CDC/CSTE pertussis case definition appendix, 1997.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Confirmed cases could be culture-positive, PCR-positive with clinical definition, or epidemiologically linked to a lab-confirmed case.

citation: CDC/CSTE pertussis 2014 case definition.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Defines clinical, laboratory, epidemiologic linkage, probable, and confirmed pertussis classifications.

citation: Skoff et al. Assessing the impact of the 2020 CSTE case definition for pertussis on reported pertussis cases. Clinical Infectious Diseases, 2024.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Estimated the 2020 definition increased reported pertussis cases by almost 10 percent.

citation: CDC. Manual for the surveillance of vaccine-preventable diseases: Chapter 10 Pertussis.
key_evidence_for_dataset: 2020 CSTE definition: all patients with cough and positive culture or PCR should be reported as confirmed.

citation: Faulkner et al. Tracking pertussis and evaluating control measures through enhanced pertussis surveillance, Emerging Infections Program, United States. Emerging Infectious Diseases, 2015.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Enhanced pertussis surveillance collected data that helped guide case definition revisions.

citation: Wang et al. Changes in outpatient visits and hospital admissions during the COVID-19 pandemic in China. BMJ Global Health, 2021.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Documented 40-60% reduction in outpatient visits during peak COVID-19 lockdown periods in China, with gradual recovery through 2021.

citation: National Health Commission of China. Statistical Bulletin on China's Health Development, 2020.
key_evidence_for_dataset: Official statistics documenting the sharp decline in healthcare utilization during the 2020 COVID-19 lockdown period.
